summary:
Ring-enhancing white matter lesions are most classically linked to granulomatous infections like tuberculosis. While toxoplasmosis can mimic this in immunosuppressed hosts, its lesions tend to be more confluent and necrotic, lacking sharp demarcation. In contrast, tuberculomas share morphological similarity with fungal or parasitic granulomas, showing central necrosis surrounded by dense inflammatory cells—an exact match for ring enhancement. Among options, toxoplasmosis is the most likely to produce similar imaging findings in immunocompromised individuals, even if slightly different in detail. Thus, although no option perfectly matches tuberculosis itself, the closest association with ring-enhancing white matter lesions is **toxoplasmosis** under certain contexts.  

Correct answer:
